name: Test Terracotta Pot
prompt: A single terracotta pot with red geraniums, medieval Italian style, isolated on a plain white background, three-quarter view, soft even studio lighting, no shadows on the background, photorealistic painted-miniature look

name: Medieval Light Post
prompt: A 13th century medieval street light post: a tall dark wrought-iron post rising from a heavy octagonal stone base, an ornate scrolled iron bracket arm at the top holding a hanging lantern with amber glass panes framed in blackened iron, warm candle glow inside. Weathered medieval Italian craftsmanship, Florence circa 1300. Single object, the entire post visible from stone base to lantern top, isolated on a plain white background, three-quarter view, soft even studio lighting, no cast shadows, painted-miniature look.
extraPrompt: No text anywhere in the image. No labels, no banners, no scrolls, no captions. The object only.

name: Duomo Floor Plan
prompt: Top-down orthographic architectural floor plan of a great Italian Gothic cathedral in the plan-shape of Santa Maria del Fiore in Florence. The plan has EXACTLY FOUR arms around ONE large central octagon, arranged like a clock face: at 12 o'clock (top) the ONLY nave � a long rectangular hall with two rows of pier dots forming side aisles, ending at a triple-door entrance facade at the very top; at 3 o'clock (right) ONE octagonal tribune ringed with small radiating chapel bays; at 6 o'clock (bottom) ONE identical octagonal tribune; at 9 o'clock (left) ONE identical octagonal tribune. Exactly three octagonal tribunes total and exactly one nave � no other arms, no second nave, no fourth tribune. Clean measured architectural drawing: thick solid dark walls, piers as filled dots, door openings as gaps, pale parchment background, high contrast, straight lines, uniform line weight. Strictly top-down, no perspective, no shading, no furniture, no people, absolutely no text or letters or numbers anywhere. Tall portrait composition, full plan visible with margins.
extraPrompt: Almost perfect � keep this exact layout, but ATTACH the three octagonal tribunes directly onto the central octagon's faces: each tribune shares its inner wall with the octagon and opens into it through a wide archway gap. No empty space between tribunes and the central octagon � the whole cathedral is one connected building.
